name: Apple Turnovers
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. In a large bowl, combine flour and salt. Mix well.
3. Add shortening and cut in until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs (about 6-8 tablespoons of water may be needed).
4. Stir in cheese and mix lightly with a fork until a dough forms.
5. Divide dough into three portions. Roll each on a floured surface into a 14 x 7-inch rectangle.
6. Cut rectangles into 2 squares, about 7 inches long.
7. In a bowl, combine brown sugar and cinnamon.
8. Fill centers of apples with the filling mixture and top each apple with butter.
9. Sprinkle remaining sugar-cinnamon mixture over pastry.
10. Place an apple in each square and fold corners to center, pinching dough tightly at top.
11. Bake for 30-35 minutes. Top with whipped cream before serving.
